OnAnimationEnd is triggered by animations inside the MudCollapse's  container

### What went wrong?

I have a `MudTable` inside `MudCollapse`. I have a `OnAnimationEnd` handler that does something.
It gets called correctly on the expansion/collapse of the panel.

/testbed
     1	﻿@namespace MudBlazor
     2	@inherits MudComponentBase
     3	
     4	<div @ontransitionend="this.AsNonRenderingEventHandler(AnimationEndAsync)"
     5	     @attributes="UserAttributes" class="@Classname" style="@Stylename">
     6	    <div class="mud-collapse-wrapper">
     7	        <div class="mud-collapse-wrapper-inner">
     8	            @ChildContent
     9	        </div>
    10	    </div>
    11	</div>

--- cs ---
     1	﻿using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components;
     2	using MudBlazor.State;
     3	using MudBlazor.Utilities;
     4	
     5	namespace MudBlazor
     6	{
     7	    /// <summary>
     8	    /// A container for content which can be collapsed and expanded.
     9	    /// </summary>
    10	    /// <seealso cref="MudExpansionPanels"/>
    11	    /// <seealso cref="MudExpansionPanel"/>
    12	    public partial class MudCollapse : MudComponentBase
    13	    {
    14	        private enum CollapseState
    15	        {
    16	            Entering, Entered, Exiting, Exited
    17	        }
    18	
    19	        private readonly ParameterState<bool> _expandedState;
    20	        private CollapseState _state = CollapseState.Exited;
    21	
    22	        protected string Classname => new CssBuilder("mud-collapse-container")
    23	            .AddClass("mud-collapse-entering", _state == CollapseState.Entering)
    24	            .AddClass("mud-collapse-entered", _state == CollapseState.Entered)
    25	            .AddClass("mud-collapse-exiting", _state == CollapseState.Exiting)
    26	            .AddClass("invisible", _state == CollapseState.Exited)
    27	            .AddClass(Class)
    28	            .Build();
    29	
    30	        protected string Stylename => new StyleBuilder()
    31	            .AddStyle("max-height", MaxHeight.ToPx(), MaxHeight != null)
    32	            .AddStyle(Style)
    33	            .Build();
    34	
    35	        /// <summary>
    36	        /// Displays content within this panel.
    37	        /// </summary>
    38	        /// <remarks>
    39	        /// Defaults to <c>false</c>.
    40	        /// </remarks>
    41	        [Parameter]
    42	        public bool Expanded { get; set; }
    43	
    44	        /// <summary>
    45	        /// The maximum allowed height of this panel, in pixels.
    46	        /// </summary>
    47	        /// <remarks>
    48	        /// Defaults to <c>null</c>.
    49	        /// </remarks>
    50	        [Parameter]
    51	        public int? MaxHeight { get; set; }
    52	
    53	        /// <summary>
    54	        /// The content within this panel.
    55	        /// </summary>
    56	        [Parameter]
    57	        public RenderFragment? ChildContent { get; set; }
    58	
    59	        /// <summary>
    60	        /// Occurs when the collapse or expand animation has finished.
    61	        /// </summary>
    62	        [Parameter]
    63	        public EventCallback OnAnimationEnd { get; set; }
    64	
    65	        /// <summary>
    66	        /// Occurs when the <see cref="Expanded"/> property has changed.
    67	        /// </summary>
    68	        [Parameter]
    69	        public EventCallback<bool> ExpandedChanged { get; set; }
    70	
    71	        public MudCollapse()
    72	        {
    73	            using var register = CreateRegisterScope();
    74	            _expandedState = register.RegisterParameter<bool>(nameof(Expanded))
    75	                .WithParameter(() => Expanded)
    76	                .WithEventCallback(() => ExpandedChanged)
    77	                .WithChangeHandler(OnExpandedParameterChangedAsync);
    78	        }
    79	
    80	        protected override void OnAfterRender(bool firstRender)
    81	        {
    82	            base.OnAfterRender(firstRender);
    83	
    84	            if (firstRender && _expandedState.Value)
    85	            {
    86	                _state = CollapseState.Entered;
    87	                StateHasChanged();
    88	            }
    89	        }
    90	
    91	        private Task OnExpandedParameterChangedAsync(ParameterChangedEventArgs<bool> args)
    92	        {
    93	            _state = args.Value ? CollapseState.Entering : CollapseState.Exiting;
    94	
    95	            return Task.CompletedTask;
    96	        }
    97	
    98	        private Task AnimationEndAsync()
    99	        {
   100	            if (_state == CollapseState.Entering)
   101	            {
   102	                _state = CollapseState.Entered;
   103	                StateHasChanged();
   104	            }
   105	            else if (_state == CollapseState.Exiting)
   106	            {
   107	                _state = CollapseState.Exited;
   108	                StateHasChanged();
   109	            }
   110	
   111	            return OnAnimationEnd.InvokeAsync(_expandedState.Value);
   112	        }
   113	    }
   114	}

     5	using AngleSharp.Dom;
     6	using AwesomeAssertions;
     7	using Bunit;
     8	using MudBlazor.UnitTests.TestComponents.Collapse;
     9	using NUnit.Framework;
    10	
    11	namespace MudBlazor.UnitTests.Components
    12	{
    13	    [TestFixture]
    14	    public class CollapseTests : BunitTest
    15	    {
    16	        [Test]
    17	        public async Task Collapse_TwoWayBinding_Test1()
    18	        {
    19	            var comp = Context.Render<CollapseBindingTest>();
    20	            var collapse = comp.FindComponent<MudCollapse>();
    21	
    22	            collapse.Markup.Should().Contain("mud-collapse-entered");
    23	
    24	            IElement Button() => comp.Find("#outside_btn");
    25	
    26	            IRenderedComponent<MudSwitch<bool>> MudSwitch() => comp.FindComponent<MudSwitch<bool>>();
    27	            // Initial state is expanded
    28	            MudSwitch().Find("input").HasAttribute("checked").Should().BeTrue();
    29	
    30	            // Collapse via button
    31	            await Button().ClickAsync();
    32	            MudSwitch().Find("input").HasAttribute("checked").Should().BeFalse();
    33	
    34	            // Expand via button
    35	            await Button().ClickAsync();
    36	            MudSwitch().Find("input").HasAttribute("checked").Should().BeTrue();
    37	
    38	            // Collapse via switch
    39	            await MudSwitch().Find("input").ChangeAsync(false);
    40	            MudSwitch().Find("input").HasAttribute("checked").Should().BeFalse();
    41	
    42	            // Expand via switch
    43	            await MudSwitch().Find("input").ChangeAsync(true);
    44	            MudSwitch().Find("input").HasAttribute("checked").Should().BeTrue();
    45	        }
    46	    }
    47	}

information.\n\n/**\n * Factory that resolves elements and manages MudKeyInterceptor instances.\n * Exposes connect/update/disconnect entry points for .NET interop.\n */\nclass MudKeyInterceptorFactory {\n    /**\n     * Creates (or reuses) a key interceptor for an element and attaches handlers.\n     */\n    connect(dotNetRef, elementId, options) {\n        //console.log('[MudBlazor | MudKeyInterceptorFactory] connect ', { dotNetRef, element, options });\n        if (!elementId)\n            throw \"elementId: expected element id!\";\n        const element = document.getElementById(elementId);\n        if (!element)\n            throw \"no element found for id: \" + elementId;\n        if (!element.mudKeyInterceptor)\n            element.mudKeyInterceptor = new MudKeyInterceptor(dotNetRef, options);\n        element.mudKeyInterceptor.connect(element);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Updates the key option for an existing interceptor registration.\n     */\n    updatekey(elementId, option) {\n        const element = document.getElementById(elementId);\n        if (!element?.mudKeyInterceptor)\n            return;\n        element.mudKeyInterceptor.updatekey(option);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Detaches a key interceptor from an element.\n     */\n    disconnect(elementId) {\n        const element = document.getElementById(elementId);\n        if (!element?.mudKeyInterceptor)\n            return;\n        element.mudKeyInterceptor.disconnect();\n    }\n}\nwindow.mudKeyInterceptor = new MudKeyInterceptorFactory();\n\n/**\n * Applies key options and raises keyboard callbacks to .NET.\n * Handles preventDefault/stopPropagation in JS before component handlers run.\n */\nclass MudKeyInterceptor {\n    constructor(dotNetRef, options) {\n        this._dotNetRef = dotNetRef;\n        this._options = options;\n        this.logger = options.enableLogging ? console.log : () => { };\n        this.logger('[MudBlazor | KeyInterceptor] Interceptor initialized', { options });\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Starts key interception on the target element (or matching child elements).\n     */\n    connect(element) {\n        if (!this._options)\n            return;\n        if (!this._options.keys)\n            throw \"_options.keys: array of KeyOptions expected\";\n        if (this._isConnected) {\n            // don't do double registration\n            return;\n        }\n        this._isConnected = true;\n        this._element = element;\n        const targetClass = this._options.targetClass;\n        // changes to the DOM subtree only require observation when targeting child elements for target class\n        if (targetClass) {\n            this.logger('[MudBlazor | KeyInterceptor] Start observing DOM of element for changes to child with class ', { element, targetClass });\n            this._observer = new MutationObserver(this.onDomChanged);\n            this._observer.mudKeyInterceptor = this;\n            this._observer.observe(this._element, { attributes: false, childList: true, subtree: true });\n        }\n        this._observedChildren = [];\n        // transform key options into a key lookup\n        this._keyOptions = {};\n        this._regexOptions = [];\n        for (const keyOption of this._options.keys) {\n            if (!keyOption?.key) {\n                this.logger('[MudBlazor | KeyInterceptor] got invalid key options: ', keyOption);\n                continue;\n            }\n            this.setKeyOption(keyOption);\n        }\n        this.logger('[MudBlazor | KeyInterceptor] key options: ', this._keyOptions);\n        if (this._regexOptions.size > 0)\n            this.logger('[MudBlazor | KeyInterceptor] regex options: ', this._regexOptions);\n        // register handlers\n        if (targetClass) {\n            for (const child of this._element.getElementsByClassName(targetClass)) {\n                this.attachHandlers(child);\n            }\n        } else {\n            this.attachHandlers(this._element);\n        }\n false;\n        if (option === \"any\")\n            return true;\n        const shift = args.shiftKey;\n        const ctrl = args.ctrlKey;\n        const alt = args.altKey;\n        const meta = args.metaKey;\n        const any = shift || ctrl || alt || meta;\n        if (any && option === \"key+any\")\n            return true;\n        if (!any && option.includes(\"key+none\"))\n (this.matchesKeyCombination(keyOptions.preventDown, args))\n            args.preventDefault();\n        if (this.matchesKeyCombination(keyOptions.stopDown, args))\n   echo "PASS: transitionend propagation is stopped at the collapse container."

EOF

cat > /tmp/repro-collapse.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
cd /testbed
printf 'Checking current MudCollapse transition handler behavior...\n'
razor=src/MudBlazor/Components/Collapse/MudCollapse.razor
code=src/MudBlazor/Components/Collapse/MudCollapse.razor.cs
if ! grep -q '@ontransitionend:stopPropagation="true"' "$razor"; then
  echo "FAIL: descendant transitionend events are not stopped before reaching MudCollapse's container handler."
  exit 1
fi
if grep -q 'return OnAnimationEnd.InvokeAsync(_expandedState.Value);' "$code"; then
  echo "FAIL: OnAnimationEnd is invoked even when MudCollapse is not entering/exiting."
  exit 1
fi
if ! grep -q 'isAnimating ? OnAnimationEnd.InvokeAsync(_expandedState.Value) : Task.CompletedTask' "$code"; then
  echo "FAIL: expected OnAnimationEnd to be gated to collapse/expand transitions."
  exit 1
fi
echo "PASS: descendant transition events are isolated and OnAnimationEnd only fires for collapse/expand transitions."
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/repro-collapse.sh
/tmp/repro-collapse.sh

2026-05-29 08:51:39,211 - INFO - swea-agent - 🤖 MODEL INPUT
OBSERVATION:

#!/usr/bin/env python3

import argparse
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess
import sys
import os
import io

from registry import registry


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Submit changes for review")
    parser.add_argument("-f", "--force", action="store_true", help="Force submit without review")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    repo_root = registry.get("ROOT", os.getenv("ROOT"))
    assert repo_root

    patch_path = Path("/root/model.patch")

    subprocess.run(
        f"git add -A && git diff --cached > {patch_path}",
        shell=True,
        stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
        stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
        cwd=repo_root,
    )

    patch = patch_path.read_text(errors="backslashreplace")

    submit_review_messages = registry.get("SUBMIT_REVIEW_MESSAGES", [])
    n_stages = len(submit_review_messages)
    current_stage = registry.get("SUBMIT_STAGE", 0)
    if not args.force and current_stage != n_stages:
        message = submit_review_messages[current_stage]
        message = message.replace("{{diff}}", patch)
        message = message.replace("{{problem_statement}}", registry.get("PROBLEM_STATEMENT", ""))
        registry["SUBMIT_STAGE"] = current_stage + 1
        print(message)
        sys.exit(0)

    print("<<SWE_AGENT_SUBMISSION>>")
    print(patch)
    print("<<SWE_AGENT_SUBMISSION>>")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # There are some super strange "ascii can't decode x" errors when printing to the terminal
    # that can be solved with setting the default encoding for stdout
    # (note that python3.6 doesn't have the reconfigure method)
    sys.stdout = io.TextIOWrapper(sys.stdout.buffer, encoding="utf-8")
    main()
